what_it_does: React region component that shows a filtered self-modification audit log for an indicated ui:// element: it lists self-apply and revert commits whose changed files touch the element's code scope, exposes per-row revert through the existing operator-only revert gate, and renders three explicit trust states for stale corpus, missing code scope, and true empty results.
observations: Only renders when `indicated` exists and starts with `ui://`.
Uses kit components `SectionHead` and `Badge`, plus `relTime` from `../api`.
Implements a three-way fail-loud state: stale corpus warning, no-code-scope message, or resolved-scope change list.
Lists changes in `.ev-list.hist-list` rows using shared `.ev`, `.ev-k`, `.ev-s`, `.ev-t` token classes.
Per-row revert button uses `.b.ghost.sm` and calls `revertSelfChangeAt(c.sha)` only for non-revert rows.
Sets `data-ui-ref="ui://workshop/self-changes"` on the container and the revert button.
Inline comments reference L5 §21.7#5, rule 4 stale trichotomy, rule 9 form, and WAVE-? REGION-BATCH-2 head-only conversion.
standouts: Explicit fail-loud stale/no-scope/empty trichotomy instead of a silent empty list.
Reuses existing event-row vocabulary and the existing operator-only revert gate rather than inventing new UI or API paths.
HEAD-only conversion pattern: title/kicker/Badge header while preserving the shared change-row body.
conventions: Uses design-system kit components and token classes (`SectionHead`, `Badge`, `.ev-*`, `.hist-*`).
Reuses shared Activity/History/Versions event-row styling for coherence.
Keeps revert bounded by reusing the existing `/api/revert` gate.
Heavy inline rule-numbered documentation explaining intent and constraints.
concerns: `changes.map((c: any, ...)` uses an `any` type for change rows.
`selfChanges` shape is inferred only through optional chaining; no local interface is visible.
Key fallback `c.sha ?? i` could reuse keys if `sha` is missing or duplicated.
`revertSelfChangeAt` signature and operator-only gating are not visible in this file.
The same `data-ui-ref` value is placed on both the region container and the revert button.
notes: A scoped self-change audit region that tells the operator loudly when the corpus join is stale or the address has no code scope, and otherwise reuses the existing event-row and revert vocabulary.
questions: What is the exact runtime type of `selfChanges` and where is it produced?
How is `stale` determined and what regenerates `design/_system`?
What is the implementation and authorization logic of `revertSelfChangeAt`?
Are `matched_files` already filtered server-side to the indicated element's scope?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it locates self-changes at an indicated ui:// element, surfaces trust states, and reuses the existing revert path.
intention_signals: To give operators a trustworthy, element-scoped view of what the system changed in the code behind a UI address, while making uncertainty explicit and keeping revert actions safe and bounded.
novelty: The stale-corpus/no-code-scope/true-empty trichotomy and the deliberate reuse of existing event-row and revert affordances for a self-change audit view.
contribution: A scoped, trust-state-aware self-change audit region that reuses existing event-row styling and the existing operator-only revert affordance, keeping the operator informed when the corpus join is stale or the address maps to no code scope.
summary_for_embedding: SelfChanges.tsx is a React region that renders a filtered self-modification audit log for an indicated ui:// element, with explicit stale-corpus, no-scope, and empty states and per-row revert via an existing operator-only gate.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a bounded, trustworthy UI for locating and reverting system self-changes that touch the code scope of a specific indicated UI element.

symbols.does: React component that renders the self-change audit log for an indicated ui:// element.
symbols.description: SelfChanges consumes the indicated UI address and self-change join state from `useApp`, renders a `SectionHead` with the address as kicker and a state badge, then displays one of three explicit trust states (stale corpus, no code scope, or resolved scope) followed by a list of self-apply/revert event rows that reuse the shared `.ev`/`.hist-*` vocabulary and offer per-row revert through the existing operator-only gate.
